proposal.reasoning: This is the first iteration in the exploration phase with no prior data. To efficiently map the parameter space and identify promising regions for the target properties (density ~1.095 g/cm³, ΔH_vap ~12.6 kcal/mol, surface tension ~42 mN/m, dipole moment ~3.96 D), I propose 8 diverse parameter sets spanning different physical regimes: (1) Baseline mid-range values for reference, (2) Strong cohesion (high ε, small σ) to test high density/H_vap scenarios, (3) Weak cohesion (low ε, large σ) for low density exploration, (4) Stiff/rigid molecular geometry (high force constants, linear angles), (5) Flexible geometry (low force constants, bent angles), (6) Large molecular size (long bonds, large rmin), (7) Compact size (short bonds, small rmin), and (8) A mixed extreme case combining strong MS2-OC interactions with weak OC interactions and asymmetric charges to test sensitivity to heterogeneous interaction patterns.

hypothesis.scientific_rationale: The target properties suggest a liquid with moderate cohesion (density ~1.095, H_vap ~12.6). By systematically varying Lennard-Jones epsilon (controlling attraction strength) from weak (-0.6) to strong (-1.8) and rmin (controlling molecular size) from compact (4.7) to expanded (5.8), we test the hypothesis that stronger intermolecular attractions increase density and heat of vaporization while smaller rmin values enhance packing efficiency. Additionally, varying charge magnitudes (0.3 to 0.7) modulates the dipole moment and electrostatic contributions to surface tension, while varying bond/angle stiffness (kb 15-55, ktheta 15-45) tests how molecular rigidity affects surface tension and dynamic properties.
hypothesis.expected_benefit: This exploration will identify whether the system behaves as a normal liquid under strong cohesive forces (Fork 2) requiring high epsilon values to reach target density, or if specific geometric configurations (Forks 4-7) dominate the thermodynamic properties. Forks 2 and 3 specifically test the density-H_vap correlation, while Forks 4 and 5 test the impact of molecular flexibility on surface tension. The results will guide refinement toward regions where ε ≈ -1.2 to -1.5 and rmin ≈ 5.0-5.3 are expected to yield optimal liquid properties.
hypothesis.test_method: The hypothesis will be tested by evaluating all 8 parameter sets in parallel MD simulations, measuring density, heat of vaporization, surface tension, and dipole moment to identify which combinations produce stable liquid behavior matching the target properties within acceptable tolerances.
